1→# External Agents for Entire CLI
     2→
     3→This repository contains standalone external agent binaries that extend the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli) with support for additional AI coding agents.
     4→
     5→## What Are External Agents?
     6→
     7→External agents are standalone binaries (named `entire-agent-<name>`) that teach Entire CLI how to work with AI coding agents it doesn't natively support. When an external agent is installed on your `PATH`, Entire discovers it automatically and gains the ability to:
     8→
     9→- **Create checkpoints** during AI coding sessions so you can rewind mistakes
    10→- **Capture transcripts** of what the AI agent did and why
    11→- **Install hooks** so the AI agent's lifecycle events (start, stop, commit) flow through Entire
    12→
    13→External agents communicate with Entire CLI via subcommands that accept and return JSON over stdin/stdout. See the [external agent protocol spec](https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md) for the full interface.
    14→
    15→## Available Agents
    16→
    17→| Agent | Directory | Status |
    18→|-------|-----------|--------|
    19→| [Kiro](agents/entire-agent-kiro/) | `agents/entire-agent-kiro/` | Implemented — hooks + transcript analysis |
    20→
    21→## Building a New External Agent
    22→
    23→This repo includes a skill that guides you through building a new external agent using an E2E-first TDD pipeline. The skill runs in three phases:
    24→
    25→1. **Research** — analyzes the target AI agent's file formats, session layout, and hook mechanisms
    26→2. **Write tests** — generates E2E and unit tests against the external agent protocol
    27→3. **Implement** — builds the Go binary to pass all tests
    28→
    29→### Getting Started — Zero Setup
    30→
    31→Clone the repo and open it in your AI coding tool. Each tool auto-discovers the skill with no additional configuration:
    32→
    33→| Tool | How it discovers | What to say |
    34→|------|-----------------|-------------|
    35→| **Claude Code** | `.claude/skills/` directory | `/entire-external-agent` |
    36→| **Codex** | `AGENTS.md` at project root | "Build an external agent" |
    37→| **Cursor** | `.cursor/rules/` directory | "Build an external agent" |
    38→| **OpenCode** | `.opencode/plugins/` auto-loaded | "Build an external agent" |
    39→
    40→The skill files live in `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/` if you want to read the details.
    41→
    42→## E2E Tests
    43→
    44→The `e2e/` directory contains a shared test harness that exercises all external agents. Tests are split into two tiers:
    45→
    46→- **Subcommand tests** (`kiro_test.go`) — exercise each protocol subcommand directly against the agent binary (identity, sessions, transcript, hooks, transcript analysis). These run without any external dependencies beyond the agent binary itself.
    47→- **Lifecycle tests** (`kiro_lifecycle_test.go`) — exercise the full integration flow: `entire enable`, agent prompt execution, git commit, checkpoint creation, and rewind. These require the `entire` CLI and the agent's own CLI (e.g. `kiro-cli-chat`) to be available.
    48→
    49→### Running Tests
    50→
    51→```bash
    52→# Run all E2E tests (subcommand-level only; lifecycle tests skip if deps missing)
    53→make test-e2e
    54→
    55→# Run lifecycle tests (fails instead of skipping if entire/kiro-cli-chat are missing)
    56→make test-e2e-lifecycle
    57→
    58→# Run unit tests for all agents
    59→make test-unit
    60→
    61→# Run everything
    62→make test-all
    63→```
    64→
    65→### Test Harness Architecture
    66→
    67→The shared harness auto-discovers and builds all agents in `agents/` via `TestMain`:
    68→
    69→| File | Purpose |
    70→|------|---------|
    71→| `e2e/setup_test.go` | `TestMain` entry point — discovers agents, builds binaries, configures PATH |
    72→| `e2e/testenv.go` | `TestEnv` — isolated filesystem environment with agent binary runner |
    73→| `e2e/harness.go` | `AgentRunner` — executes agent subcommands, captures stdout/stderr/exit code |
    74→| `e2e/fixtures.go` | Test input builders: `HookInput`, `ParseHookInput`, `KiroTranscript` |
    75→| `e2e/entire.go` | CLI wrappers: `EntireEnable`, `EntireDisable`, `EntireRewindList`, `EntireRewind` |
    76→| `e2e/lifecycle.go` | `LifecycleEnv` — full lifecycle environment (git repo + `entire enable` + checkpoint helpers) |
    77→
    78→### Environment Variables
    79→
    80→| Variable | Description |
    81→|----------|-------------|
    82→| `E2E_ENTIRE_BIN` | Path to the `entire` binary (defaults to `entire` from PATH) |
    83→| `E2E_REQUIRE_LIFECYCLE` | Set to `1` to fail (instead of skip) when lifecycle dependencies are missing |
    84→
    85→## Repository Layout
    86→
    87→```
    88→agents/                          # Standalone external agent projects
    89→  entire-agent-kiro/             # Kiro agent (Go binary)
    90→e2e/                             # Shared E2E test harness for all agents
    91→.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/  # Skill files (research, test-writer, implementer)
    92→AGENTS.md                        # Codex auto-discovery
    93→.cursor/rules/                   # Cursor auto-discovery
    94→.opencode/plugins/               # OpenCode auto-discovery
    95→```
    96→

1→# External Agent Builder
     2→
     3→This repository includes a skill that guides you through building standalone external agent binaries for the [Entire CLI](https://github.com/entireio/cli). The skill uses E2E-first TDD and runs in three phases.
     4→
     5→## Available Commands
     6→
     7→| Command | Skill file | Description |
     8→|---------|-----------|-------------|
     9→| Full pipeline | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` | Run all three phases sequentially |
    10→| Research | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md` | Analyze the target agent's capabilities and map to the protocol |
    11→| Write tests | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md` | Scaffold the binary and create E2E test harness |
    12→| Implement | `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md` | Build the binary using E2E-first TDD (unit tests last) |
    13→
    14→## How to Use
    15→
    16→When the user asks to "build an external agent", "create an agent binary", or "external agent plugin":
    17→
    18→1. Read `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md` for the full pipeline overview
    19→2. Follow the three phases in order: research, write-tests, implement
    20→3. Each phase has a dedicated skill file with detailed instructions
    21→
    22→## Tool Mapping (Codex)
    23→
    24→The skill files reference Claude Code tool names. Map them to Codex equivalents:
    25→
    26→| Skill references | Codex equivalent |
    27→|-----------------|-----------------|
    28→| `TodoWrite` | `update_plan` |
    29→| `Task` / subagent launches | Codex's native subagent system |
    30→| `Skill` tool | Read the skill file directly |
    31→| `Read`, `Write`, `Edit`, `Glob`, `Grep`, `Bash` | Native Codex tools |
    32→
    33→## Protocol Spec
    34→
    35→The external agent protocol is defined at:
    36→`https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`
    37→

1→# E2E Tests
     2→
     3→End-to-end tests for external agents, exercising the full lifecycle: agent prompts, git hooks, checkpoints, and rewind.
     4→
     5→## Structure
     6→
     7→```
     8→e2e/
     9→├── agents/           # Agent interface + implementations (kiro, etc.)
    10→│   ├── agent.go      # Agent/Session interfaces, registry, concurrency gating
    11→│   ├── tmux.go       # TmuxSession for interactive PTY sessions
    12→│   └── kiro.go       # Kiro agent (kiro-cli-chat)
    13→├── entire/           # `entire` CLI wrapper
    14→│   └── entire.go     # Enable, Disable, RewindList, Rewind
    15→├── testutil/         # Shared test infrastructure
    16→│   ├── metadata.go   # Checkpoint/session metadata types
    17→│   ├── artifacts.go  # Artifact capture (git-log, pane, logs)
    18→│   ├── repo.go       # RepoState, SetupRepo, ForEachAgent, Git helpers
    19→│   └── assertions.go # Test assertions (testify-based)
    20→├── bootstrap/        # Pre-test agent bootstrap (CI auth setup)
    21→│   └── main.go       # go run ./e2e/bootstrap
    22→├── setup_test.go     # TestMain: build agents, artifact dir, preflight
    23→├── kiro_lifecycle_test.go  # Lifecycle tests (ForEachAgent pattern)
    24→├── kiro_test.go      # Protocol-level tests (stdin/stdout subcommands)
    25→├── harness.go        # AgentRunner for protocol tests
    26→├── testenv.go        # TestEnv for protocol tests
    27→└── fixtures.go       # HookInput, KiroTranscript builders
    28→```
    29→
    30→## Running Tests
    31→
    32→### All E2E tests (protocol + lifecycle)
    33→
    34→```bash
    35→make test-e2e
    36→```
    37→
    38→### Lifecycle tests only
    39→
    40→```bash
    41→make test-e2e-lifecycle
    42→```
    43→
    44→### Single test
    45→
    46→```bash
    47→cd e2e && go test -tags=e2e -v -count=1 -run TestLifecycle_SinglePromptManualCommit ./...
    48→```
    49→
    50→## Environment Variables
    51→
    52→| Variable | Description |
    53→|----------|-------------|
    54→| `E2E_AGENT` | Filter to a single agent (e.g. `kiro`). Default: all registered agents. |
    55→| `E2E_ENTIRE_BIN` | Path to `entire` binary. Falls back to `$PATH` lookup. |
    56→| `E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR` | Override artifact output directory. |
    57→| `E2E_KEEP_REPOS` | Set to any value to preserve temp repos after tests. |
    58→| `E2E_REQUIRE_LIFECYCLE` | Set to `1` to fail (not skip) when lifecycle deps are missing. |
    59→| `E2E_CONCURRENT_TEST_LIMIT` | Override per-agent concurrency limit (default: 2 for kiro). |
    60→
    61→## Adding a New Agent
    62→
    63→1. Create `e2e/agents/<name>.go` implementing the `Agent` interface.
    64→2. In `init()`, conditionally register based on `E2E_AGENT` env var.
    65→3. Call `RegisterGate("<name>", N)` to set concurrency limit.
    66→4. If it's an external agent, implement `ExternalAgent` interface.
    67→
    68→## Debugging Failures
    69→
    70→After a test run, check `e2e/artifacts/<timestamp>/` for:
    71→
    72→- `git-log.txt` — full git history including checkpoint branch
    73→- `git-tree.txt` — file tree at HEAD and checkpoint branch tip
    74→- `console.log` — all agent prompts, outputs, and git commands
    75→- `pane.txt` — final tmux pane content (interactive tests)
    76→- `PASS` / `FAIL` — test outcome marker
    77→- `checkpoint-metadata/` — checkpoint and session metadata JSON
    78→- `entire-logs/` — entire CLI debug logs
    79→
    80→Set `E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1` to preserve the temp git repo (symlinked from artifact dir).

    23→func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
    24→	tmpDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "e2e-agents-*")
    25→	if err != nil {
    26→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "failed to create temp dir: %v\n", err)
    27→		os.Exit(1)
    28→	}
    29→	defer os.RemoveAll(tmpDir)
    30→
    31→	discoveredAgents, err := discoverAgents()
    32→	if err != nil {
    33→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "failed to discover agents: %v\n", err)
    34→		os.Exit(1)
    35→	}
    36→
    37→	if len(discoveredAgents) == 0 {
    38→		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "no agents found in agents/ directory")
    39→		os.Exit(1)
    40→	}
    41→
    42→	for _, agentDir := range discoveredAgents {
    43→		agentName := filepath.Base(agentDir)
    44→		binPath := filepath.Join(tmpDir, agentName)
    45→		// Each agent has its own go.mod, so build from the agent's directory
    46→		agentAbsDir := filepath.Join(repoRoot(), agentDir)
    47→		mainPkg := "./cmd/" + agentName
    48→
    49→		fmt.Printf("Building %s...\n", agentName)
    50→		cmd := exec.Command("go", "build", "-o", binPath, mainPkg)
    51→		cmd.Dir = agentAbsDir
    52→		cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    53→		cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
    54→		if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
    55→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "failed to build %s: %v\n", agentName, err)
    56→			os.Exit(1)
    57→		}
    58→
    59→		agentBinaries[agentName] = binPath
    60→		fmt.Printf("Built %s -> %s\n", agentName, binPath)
    61→	}
    62→
    63→	// Add the temp bin directory to PATH so that `entire enable` can discover
    64→	// the agent binaries (e.g. entire-agent-kiro) during lifecycle tests.
    65→	os.Setenv("PATH", tmpDir+string(os.PathListSeparator)+os.Getenv("PATH"))
    66→
    67→	// --- Artifact directory setup ---
    68→	runDir := os.Getenv("E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR")
    69→	if runDir == "" {
    70→		_, file, _, _ := runtime.Caller(0)
    71→		testutil.ArtifactRoot = filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(file), "artifacts")
    72→		runDir = testutil.ArtifactRunDir()
    73→	}
    74→	_ = os.MkdirAll(runDir, 0o755)
    75→	testutil.SetRunDir(runDir)
    76→
    77→	// Prepend the entire binary's directory to PATH so git hooks resolve
    78→	// to the same binary the test harness uses.
    79→	entireBin := entire.BinPath()
    80→	if dir := filepath.Dir(entireBin); dir != "." {
    81→		os.Setenv("PATH", dir+string(os.PathListSeparator)+os.Getenv("PATH"))
    82→	}
    83→
    84→	// --- Preflight checks ---
    85→	if _, err := exec.LookPath("tmux"); err != nil {
    86→		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "warning: tmux not found — interactive session tests will fail")
    87→	}
    88→
    89→	// Write entire version info to artifact dir.
    90→	version := "unknown"
    91→	if out, err := exec.Command(entireBin, "version").Output(); err == nil {
    92→		version = string(out)
    93→	}
    94→	preflight := fmt.Sprintf("entire binary:  %s\nentire version: %s\n", entireBin, version)
    95→	_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(runDir, "entire-version.txt"), []byte(preflight), 0o644)
    96→
    97→	// Isolate git config to prevent user's ~/.gitconfig from interfering.
    98→	os.Setenv("GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL", "/dev/null")
    99→
   100→	os.Exit(m.Run())
   101→}

   103→func discoverAgents() ([]string, error) {
   104→	agentsDir := filepath.Join(repoRoot(), "agents")
   105→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(agentsDir)
   106→	if err != nil {
   107→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("read agents dir: %w", err)
   108→	}
   109→
   110→	var agentDirs []string
   111→	for _, entry := range entries {
   112→		if !entry.IsDir() {
   113→			continue
   114→		}
   115→		if !strings.HasPrefix(entry.Name(), "entire-agent-") {
   116→			continue
   117→		}
   118→		// Verify it has a cmd/<name>/main.go
   119→		mainFile := filepath.Join(agentsDir, entry.Name(), "cmd", entry.Name(), "main.go")
   120→		if _, err := os.Stat(mainFile); err != nil {
   121→			continue
   122→		}
   123→		agentDirs = append(agentDirs, filepath.Join("agents", entry.Name()))
   124→	}
   125→	return agentDirs, nil
   126→}
   127→
   128→func repoRoot() string {
   129→	// Walk up from e2e/ to find the repo root
   130→	dir, err := os.Getwd()
   131→	if err != nil {
   132→		return ".."
   133→	}
   134→	// If we're in the e2e directory, go up one level
   135→	if filepath.Base(dir) == "e2e" {
   136→		return filepath.Dir(dir)
   137→	}
   138→	return dir
   139→}

     5→import (
     6→	"context"
     7→	"os"
     8→	"path/filepath"
     9→	"testing"
    10→	"time"
    11→
    12→	"github.com/entireio/external-agents/e2e/entire"
    13→	"github.com/entireio/external-agents/e2e/testutil"
    14→	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"

    17→
    18→// TestLifecycle_SinglePromptManualCommit verifies the basic flow:
    19→// agent creates a file → git add + commit → checkpoint exists with trailer.
    20→func TestLifecycle_SinglePromptManualCommit(t *testing.T) {
    21→	testutil.ForEachAgent(t, 2*time.Minute, func(t *testing.T, s *testutil.RepoState, ctx context.Context) {
    22→		_, err := s.RunPrompt(t, ctx, "Create a file called hello.txt with the content 'hello world'. Do not ask for confirmation.")
    23→		require.NoError(t, err, "prompt failed")
    24→
    25→		testutil.AssertFileExists(t, s.Dir, "hello.txt")
    26→
    27→		s.Git(t, "add", ".")
    28→		s.Git(t, "commit", "-m", "add hello.txt")
    29→
    30→		testutil.WaitForCheckpoint(t, s, 30*time.Second)
    31→
    32→		cpID := testutil.AssertHasCheckpointTrailer(t, s.Dir, "HEAD")
    33→		testutil.AssertCheckpointExists(t, s.Dir, cpID)
    34→	})
    35→}
    36→
    37→// TestLifecycle_MultiplePromptsManualCommit sends two prompts, commits once,
    38→// and verifies the checkpoint covers both files.
    39→func TestLifecycle_MultiplePromptsManualCommit(t *testing.T) {

     2→name: entire-external-agent
     3→description: >
     4→  Run all three external agent binary phases sequentially: research, write-tests,
     5→  and implement using E2E-first TDD (unit tests written last).
     6→  Accepts an optional argument to run a single phase: research, write-tests, or implement.
     7→  Usage: /entire-external-agent [phase] — omit phase to run full pipeline.
     8→  Use when the user says "build external agent", "create agent binary",
     9→  "external agent plugin", or wants to run the full pipeline end-to-end.
    10→---
    11→
    12→# External Agent Binary — Full Pipeline
    13→
    14→Build a standalone external agent binary that implements the Entire CLI's external agent protocol using E2E-first TDD. Parameters are collected once and reused across all phases.
    15→
    16→## Parameters
    17→
    18→Collect these before starting (ask the user if not provided):
    19→
    20→| Parameter | Description | How to derive |
    21→|-----------|-------------|---------------|
    22→| `AGENT_NAME` | Human-readable name (e.g., "Windsurf") | User provides |
    23→| `AGENT_SLUG` | Binary suffix: `entire-agent-<AGENT_SLUG>` (kebab-case) | Kebab-case of agent name |
    24→| `LANGUAGE` | Implementation language (Go, Python, TypeScript, Rust) | User provides; default Go |
    25→| `PROJECT_DIR` | Where to create the project | Default: `./entire-agent-<AGENT_SLUG>` |
    26→| `CAPABILITIES` | Which optional capabilities to implement | Derived from research phase |
    27→| `ENTIRE_BIN` | Path to the Entire CLI binary | Default: `entire` from PATH, or `E2E_ENTIRE_BIN` env |
    28→
    29→## Phase Selection
    30→
    31→This skill accepts an optional argument to run a single phase:
    32→
    33→- `/entire-external-agent research` — Run only Phase 1 (research)
    34→- `/entire-external-agent write-tests` — Run only Phase 2 (scaffold + E2E tests)
    35→- `/entire-external-agent implement` — Run only Phase 3 (E2E-first TDD implementation)
    36→- `/entire-external-agent` (no argument) — Run all three phases sequentially
    37→
    38→If an argument is provided, skip directly to that phase's procedure. Parameters and prerequisites still apply — collect them before starting.
    39→
    40→## Protocol Spec
    41→
    42→Use the protocol specification at:
    43→`https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`
    44→
    45→If a user provides a different protocol spec location explicitly, use that instead and pass it to each phase as `PROTOCOL_SPEC_LOCATION`.
    46→
    47→## Core Rule: E2E-First TDD
    48→
    49→This skill enforces strict E2E-first test-driven development. The rules:
    50→
    51→1. **E2E tests are the spec.** The `e2e/` test harness defines what "working" means. The agent binary must pass all E2E tests to be considered complete.
    52→2. **Run E2E tests at every step.** Each implementation tier starts by running the E2E test and watching it fail. You implement until it passes. No exceptions.
    53→3. **Unit tests are written last.** After all E2E tiers pass, you write unit tests using real data collected from E2E runs as golden fixtures.
    54→4. **If you didn't watch it fail, you don't know if it tests the right thing.** Never write a test you haven't seen fail first.
    55→5. **Minimum viable fix.** At each E2E failure, implement only the code needed to fix that failure. Don't anticipate future tiers.
    56→
    57→## Pipeline
    58→
    59→Run these three phases in order. Each phase builds on the previous phase's output.
    60→
    61→### Phase 1: Research
    62→
    63→Discover the target agent's hook mechanism, transcript format, session management, and configuration. Map native concepts to protocol subcommands. Produces `<PROJECT_DIR>/AGENT.md` with protocol mapping and E2E prerequisites.
    64→
    65→Use the Read tool to read the file `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md` and follow the procedure it contains.
    66→
    67→**Expected output:** `<PROJECT_DIR>/AGENT.md` — agent research one-pager with protocol mapping and E2E test prerequisites.
    68→
    69→**Commit gate:** After the research phase completes, create a git commit for the resulting files.
    70→
    71→**Gate:** If the agent lacks any mechanism for lifecycle hooks or session management, discuss with the user before proceeding. Some agents may only support a subset of the protocol.
    72→
    73→### Phase 2: Write-Tests
    74→
    75→Scaffold the binary with compilable stubs and create a self-contained `e2e/` test harness in the project directory. The harness exercises the full human workflow: `entire enable`, real agent invocation, hook firing, checkpoint validation. Tests are expected to fail at this stage — they define the spec.
    76→
    77→Use the Read tool to read the file `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md` and follow the procedure it contains.
    78→
    79→**Expected output:** Complete project directory at `<PROJECT_DIR>` with compiled binary stubs and `e2e/` test harness that compiles but fails.
    80→
    81→**Commit gate:** After the scaffold compiles and the e2e harness compiles (`cd e2e && go test -c -tags=e2e`), create a git commit.
    82→
    83→### Phase 3: Implement (E2E-First, Unit Tests Last)
    84→
    85→Build the real agent binary using strict E2E-first TDD. E2E tests drive development at every step — run each tier, watch it fail, implement the minimum fix, repeat. Unit tests are written only after all E2E tiers pass, using real data from E2E runs as golden fixtures.
    86→
    87→Use the Read tool to read the file `.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md` and follow the procedure it contains.
    88→
    89→**Expected output:** Fully implemented binary where all E2E tests pass and unit tests lock in behavior.
    90→
    91→**Note:** `AGENT.md` is a living document — Phases 2 and 3 update it when they discover new information during testing or implementation.
    92→
    93→## Final Summary
    94→
    95→After all three phases, summarize:
    96→- Agent name and binary name
    97→- Language used
    98→- Capabilities declared
    99→- E2E test results (all tiers passing)
   100→- Unit test coverage
   101→- Installation instructions (`go install`, `pip install`, etc.)
   102→- Any remaining gaps or TODOs
   103→

1→# Debug External Agent E2E Failures
     2→
     3→Diagnose external agent bugs using captured artifacts from the E2E test suite. Artifacts are written to `e2e/artifacts/` locally or downloaded from CI via GitHub Actions.
     4→
     5→## Inputs
     6→
     7→The user provides either:
     8→- **A test run directory:** `e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/` -- triage all failures
     9→- **A specific test directory:** `e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/{TestName}-{agent}/` -- debug one test
    10→
    11→## Test Categories
    12→
    13→This repo has two kinds of tests:
    14→
    15→| Category | File | What it tests |
    16→|----------|------|---------------|
    17→| **Protocol tests** | `e2e/kiro_test.go` | Individual agent subcommands (`info`, `detect`, `get-session-id`, `parse-hook-event`, etc.) via `AgentRunner` |
    18→| **Lifecycle tests** | `e2e/kiro_lifecycle_test.go` | Full workflows: prompt execution → git commit → checkpoint creation, using `entire` CLI + agent CLI |
    19→
    20→Protocol tests run against the agent binary directly. Lifecycle tests require both the `entire` CLI and the agent's underlying CLI (e.g., `kiro-cli-chat`).
    21→
    22→## Artifact Layout
    23→
    24→```
    25→e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/
    26→├── entire-version.txt          # CLI version under test
    27→└── {TestName}-{agent}/         # e.g., TestLifecycle_SinglePromptManualCommit-kiro
    28→    ├── PASS or FAIL            # Status marker
    29→    ├── console.log             # Full operation transcript
    30→    ├── pane.txt                # Tmux pane capture (interactive session tests only)
    31→    ├── git-log.txt             # git log --decorate --graph --all
    32→    ├── git-tree.txt            # ls-tree HEAD + entire/checkpoints/v1 branch
    33→    ├── entire-logs/entire.log  # CLI structured JSON logs from .entire/logs/
    34→    ├── checkpoint-metadata/    # Checkpoint + session metadata
    35→    │   └── {first-2-chars}/{remaining-10-chars}/
    36→    │       ├── metadata.json   # Checkpoint-level metadata
    37→    │       └── 0/
    38→    │           └── metadata.json   # Session 0 metadata
    39→    └── repo -> /tmp/...        # Symlink to preserved repo (E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1 only)
    40→```
    41→
    42→## Preserved Repo
    43→
    44→When the test run was executed with `E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1`, each test's artifact directory contains a `repo` symlink pointing to the preserved temporary git repository. This is the actual repo the test operated on -- you can inspect it directly.
    45→
    46→**Navigate via the symlink** (e.g., `{artifact-dir}/repo/`) rather than resolving the `/tmp/...` path. The symlink lives inside the artifact directory so permissions and paths stay consistent.
    47→
    48→The preserved repo contains:
    49→- Full git history with all branches (main, `entire/checkpoints/v1`)
    50→- The `.entire/` directory with CLI state, config, and raw logs
    51→- All files the agent created or modified, in their final state
    52→
    53→This is the most powerful debugging tool -- you can run `git log`, `git diff`, `git show`, inspect `.entire/` internals, and see exactly what the CLI left behind.
    54→
    55→## Debugging Workflow
    56→
    57→### 1. Triage (if given a run directory)
    58→
    59→List the test subdirectories and check for `FAIL` markers. Read the Go test output or `console.log` files to identify failures and their error messages.
    60→
    61→### 2. Read console.log (most important)
    62→
    63→Full transcript of every operation:
    64→- `> kiro-cli-chat chat --no-interactive ...` -- agent prompts with stdout/stderr
    65→- `> git add/commit/...` -- git commands
    66→- `> send: ...` -- interactive session inputs (tmux-based tests)
    67→
    68→This tells you what happened chronologically.
    69→
    70→### 3. Read test source code
    71→
    72→Find the failing test in `e2e/kiro_lifecycle_test.go` (lifecycle tests) or `e2e/kiro_test.go` (protocol tests). Understand what the test expected vs what console.log shows actually happened.
    73→
    74→Key test infrastructure to understand:
    75→- `testutil.ForEachAgent()` -- runs the test for each registered agent with timeout
    76→- `testutil.SetupRepo()` -- creates temp git repo, runs `entire enable`, sets up artifact capture
    77→- `s.RunPrompt()` -- sends a prompt to the agent and captures output
    78→- `testutil.WaitForCheckpoint()` -- polls until checkpoint branch advances
    79→- `testutil.CaptureArtifacts()` -- called during t.Cleanup(), writes all artifacts
    80→
    81→### 4. Diagnose the issue
    82→
    83→Cross-reference console.log (what happened) with the test (what should have happened). Determine whether the issue is in the agent, the CLI, or the test itself:
    84→
    85→| Symptom | Investigation |
    86→|---------|---------------|
    87→| Agent subcommand returns wrong output | Protocol test: check `AgentRunner` invocation, compare expected vs actual JSON |
    88→| Agent prompt fails / produces wrong files | Check `console.log` for agent stderr, verify agent CLI is available |
    89→| Checkpoint not created / timeout | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for hook invocations, phase transitions, errors |
    90→| Wrong checkpoint content | Check `git-tree.txt` for checkpoint branch files, `checkpoint-metadata/` for session info |
    91→| Hooks didn't fire | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for missing hook entries (session-start, user-prompt-submit, stop, post-commit) |
    92→| Detection fails | Check agent `detect` subcommand output, verify expected files exist in repo |
    93→| Session ID wrong | Check `get-session-id` output, verify `.entire/` session state |
    94→| Attribution issues | Check `checkpoint-metadata/` for `files_touched`, session metadata for attribution data |
    95→| Strategy mismatch | Check `entire-logs/entire.log` for `strategy` field, verify auto-commit vs manual-commit behavior |
    96→| Interactive session hangs | Check `pane.txt` for tmux capture, look for agent waiting on input |
    97→
    98→### 5. Deep dive files
    99→
   100→- **console.log**: Chronological transcript of all operations. Most important file for understanding what happened.
   101→- **pane.txt**: Tmux pane capture for interactive session tests. Shows the terminal state at artifact capture time.
   102→- **entire-logs/entire.log**: Structured JSON logs -- hook lifecycle, session phases (`active` -> `idle` -> `ended`), warnings, errors. Key fields: `component`, `hook`, `strategy`, `session_id`.
   103→- **git-log.txt**: Commit graph showing main branch, `entire/checkpoints/v1`, checkpoint initialization.
   104→- **git-tree.txt**: Files at HEAD vs checkpoint branch (separated by `--- entire/checkpoints/v1 ---`).
   105→- **checkpoint-metadata/**: `metadata.json` has `checkpoint_id`, `strategy`, `files_touched`, `token_usage`, and `sessions` array. Session subdirs have per-session details including `agent`, `transcript_path`, and `initial_attribution`.
   106→
   107→### 6. Report findings
   108→
   109→Identify whether the issue is in:
   110→- **Agent binary** (protocol compliance, subcommand output, detection logic)
   111→- **Agent CLI** (kiro-cli-chat behavior, prompt execution, file creation)
   112→- **Entire CLI hooks** (prepare-commit-msg, commit-msg, post-commit)
   113→- **Session management** (phase transitions, session tracking, session IDs)
   114→- **Checkpoint creation** (branch management, metadata writing, content hash)
   115→- **Attribution** (file tracking, prompt correlation, transcript capture)
   116→- **Test harness** (testutil assertions, timing, environment setup)
   117→

    31→// SetupRepo creates a fresh git repository in a temporary directory, seeds it
    32→// with an initial commit, and runs `entire enable` for the given agent.
    33→// Artifact capture is registered as a cleanup function.
    34→func SetupRepo(t *testing.T, agent agents.Agent) *RepoState {
    35→	t.Helper()
    36→
    37→	keepRepos := os.Getenv("E2E_KEEP_REPOS") != ""
    38→
    39→	dir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "e2e-repo-*")
    40→	if err != nil {
    41→		t.Fatalf("create temp dir: %v", err)
    42→	}
    43→	if keepRepos {
    44→		t.Logf("E2E_KEEP_REPOS: repo will be preserved at %s", dir)
    45→	} else {
    46→		t.Cleanup(func() { os.RemoveAll(dir) })
    47→	}
    48→
    49→	// Resolve symlinks (macOS: /var -> /private/var) so paths match
    50→	// what agent CLIs see when they resolve their own CWD.
    51→	if resolved, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(dir); err == nil {
    52→		dir = resolved
    53→	}
    54→
    55→	Git(t, dir, "init")
    56→	Git(t, dir, "config", "user.name", "E2E Test")
    57→	Git(t, dir, "config", "user.email", "e2e@test.local")
    58→	Git(t, dir, "commit", "--allow-empty", "-m", "initial commit")
    59→
    60→	// External agents need external_agents enabled in settings before enable.
    61→	if ea, ok := agent.(agents.ExternalAgent); ok && ea.IsExternalAgent() {
    62→		entireDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".entire")
    63→		if err := os.MkdirAll(entireDir, 0o755); err != nil {
    64→			t.Fatalf("create .entire for external agent: %v", err)

    66→		if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(entireDir, "settings.json"),
    67→			[]byte("{\"external_agents\": true}\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
    68→			t.Fatalf("write external_agents setting: %v", err)
    69→		}
    70→	}
    71→
    72→	entire.Enable(t, dir, agent.EntireAgent())
    73→	PatchSettings(t, dir, map[string]any{"log_level": "debug"})

    75→	// Create artifact dir eagerly so console.log is written to disk
    76→	// incrementally. Even if the test is killed by a global timeout,
    77→	// partial output survives.
    78→	artDir := artifactDir(t)

     5→Kiro has enough hook, session, and transcript surface area to fit the Entire external-agent protocol with one logical `kiro` agent. The built-in adapter in the Kiro worktree already treats CLI and IDE as two native entrypoints that normalize into the same Entire lifecycle.
     6→
     7→## Static Checks
     8→| Check | Result | Notes |
     9→|-------|--------|-------|
    10→| Binary present | DOCS/CODE | Worktree code and docs refer to `kiro-cli-chat` as the standalone CLI used for headless auth and prompt runs |
    11→| Help available | DOCS/CODE | Worktree code and docs show CLI support for `chat`, `whoami`, `login`, and `--no-interactive` usage |
    12→| Version info | DOCS/CODE | Version output was not live-probed here; protocol mapping only requires the binary be discoverable through CLI tooling |
    13→| Hook keywords | PASS | CLI hooks, IDE hooks, prompt submit, tool use, stop |
    14→| Session keywords | PASS | session ID, session ref, resume, transcript, workspace sessions |
    15→| Config directory | PASS | `.kiro/`, `.vscode/`, and Kiro IDE workspace storage under the home directory |
    16→| Documentation | PASS | `/Users/alisha/Projects/wt/kiro-oneshot/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md` and Kiro adapter code |
    17→
    18→## Binary
    19→- Name: `kiro-cli-chat` for headless CLI usage; `kiro-cli` is the desktop wrapper that forces browser OAuth
    20→- Version: not required for protocol mapping
    21→- Install: use the Kiro CLI/desktop installation that provides `kiro-cli-chat`; the adapter expects the binary to be on `PATH`
    22→
    23→## Hook Mechanism
    24→- CLI hook config file: `.kiro/agents/entire.json`
    25→- CLI hook config format: JSON with `name`, `tools`, and nested `hooks`
    26→- CLI hook names and protocol mapping:
    27→  | Native Hook Name | When It Fires | Protocol Event Type |
    28→  |-----------------|---------------|---------------------|
    29→  | `agentSpawn` | CLI session starts | `SessionStart` |
    30→  | `userPromptSubmit` | User submits a prompt | `TurnStart` |
    31→  | `preToolUse` | Before a tool call | no lifecycle event |
    32→  | `postToolUse` | After a tool call | no lifecycle event |
    33→  | `stop` | CLI turn ends | `TurnEnd` |
    34→- CLI hook payload: JSON on stdin with `hook_event_name`, `cwd`, `prompt`, `tool_name`, `tool_input`, and `tool_response`
    35→- IDE hook config files: `.kiro/hooks/*.kiro.hook`
    36→- IDE hook config format: JSON with `enabled`, `name`, `description`, `version`, `when`, and `then`
    37→- IDE trigger types installed by Entire: `promptSubmit`, `agentStop`, `preToolUse`, `postToolUse`
    38→- IDE hook input: the adapter tolerates empty stdin and falls back to environment variables such as `USER_PROMPT`
    39→
    40→## Session Management
    41→- Session ID source: a stable Entire session ID is normally generated at `agentSpawn` and cached in `.entire/tmp/kiro-active-session`; if that cache is missing, including IDE flow where there is no IDE `agentSpawn` hook, the adapter falls back to generating and caching one during `userPromptSubmit`
    42→- Session directory: `.entire/tmp/` under the repo root
    43→- Session file format: cached JSON transcript, one file per session ID
    44→- Session file path: `.entire/tmp/<session-id>.json`
    45→- Native CLI session lookup: SQLite database at `~/Library/Application Support/kiro-cli/data.sqlite3` on macOS or `~/.local/share/kiro-cli/data.sqlite3` on Linux
    46→- Native CLI lookup key: the current working directory is queried against `conversations_v2`
    47→- Native IDE session lookup: `~/Library/Application Support/Kiro/User/globalStorage/kiro.kiroagent/workspace-sessions/<base64(cwd)>/sessions.json` on macOS or the matching `~/.config/...` path on Linux
    48→- IDE transcript source: the most recent entry in `sessions.json`, then `<sessionId>.json` in the same directory
    49→
    50→## Transcript
    51→- CLI transcript format: JSON object with `conversation_id` and `history`
    52→- CLI history entries: paired user and assistant messages
    53→- User prompt shape: `history[].user.content` containing a tagged union such as `{"Prompt":{"prompt":"..."}}`
    54→- Assistant tool-call shape: `history[].assistant` containing `{"ToolUse": {...}}`
    55→- Assistant response shape: `history[].assistant` containing `{"Response": {...}}`
    56→- IDE transcript format: JSON object with `history`, where each entry contains a `message` object
    57→- IDE message shape: Anthropic-style `role` plus `content`
    58→- CLI transcript capture: fetched from SQLite at turn end and cached to `.entire/tmp/<session-id>.json`
    59→- IDE transcript capture: copied from the workspace session file into `.entire/tmp/<session-id>.json`
    60→
    61→## Protocol Mapping
    62→| Subcommand | Native Concept | Implementation Notes | Feasibility |
    63→|-----------|---------------|---------------------|-------------|
    64→| `info` | adapter metadata | static JSON describing `kiro` and declared capabilities | Required |
    65→| `detect` | `.kiro` repository config | presence is inferred from repo-local Kiro state | Required |
    66→| `get-session-id` | cached stable session ID | use the cached Entire session ID, not Kiro's transient DB state | Required |
    67→| `get-session-dir` | Entire cache directory | return `<repo>/.entire/tmp` | Required |
    68→| `resolve-session-file` | normalized cache path | return `<repo>/.entire/tmp/<id>.json` | Required |
    69→| `read-session` | cached transcript file | read the normalized JSON cache and synthesize `AgentSession` | Required |
    70→| `write-session` | normalized transcript cache | write the cached transcript back for rewind/resume | Required |
    71→| `read-transcript` | cached transcript bytes | return raw bytes from `.entire/tmp/<id>.json` | Required |
    72→| `chunk-transcript` | raw transcript bytes | chunk the cached JSON for transport; no native Kiro format change needed | Required |
    73→| `reassemble-transcript` | chunk reassembly | reverse `chunk-transcript` | Required |
    74→| `format-resume-command` | Kiro resume command | return `kiro-cli chat --resume` | Required |
    75→| `parse-hook` | CLI/IDE hook payloads | map CLI stdin JSON or IDE env-based input to lifecycle events | If hooks capable |
    76→| `install-hooks` | `.kiro/agents/entire.json`, `.kiro/hooks/*.kiro.hook`, `.vscode/settings.json` | install both CLI and IDE support in one operation | If hooks capable |
    77→| `uninstall-hooks` | reverse hook installation | remove Entire-owned CLI and IDE hook files plus trusted commands | If hooks capable |
    78→| `are-hooks-installed` | config presence check | true when either CLI hooks or IDE hooks are present | If hooks capable |
    79→| `get-transcript-position` | transcript length | CLI uses history entry count; IDE uses history count from cached JSON | If transcript analyzer |
    80→| `extract-modified-files` | transcript tool-use history | parse tool calls and file edits from cached transcript | If transcript analyzer |
    81→| `extract-prompts` | user prompt history | return prompt text from transcript history | If transcript analyzer |
    82→| `extract-summary` | last assistant response | return the final assistant response text | If transcript analyzer |
    83→
    84→## Selected Capabilities
    85→| Capability | Declared | Justification |
    86→|-----------|----------|---------------|
    87→| hooks | true | Kiro has both CLI hook config and IDE hook files |
    88→| transcript_analyzer | true | Kiro transcripts are JSON and can be parsed for prompts and file changes |
    89→| transcript_preparer | false | the adapter only needs to read cached transcripts, not pre-process live files |
    90→| token_calculator | false | the worktree adapter does not expose a token-counting path |
    91→| text_generator | false | Kiro's CLI is used for agent execution, not metadata generation |
    92→| hook_response_writer | false | the adapter does not depend on structured hook responses |
    93→| subagent_aware_extractor | false | the worktree code does not model a subagent transcript tree |
    94→
    95→## Gaps & Limitations
    96→- Kiro CLI and Kiro IDE do not share the same native storage format, so Entire must normalize them into `.entire/tmp/<session-id>.json`
    97→- `kiro-cli` is not the right binary for headless support; the adapter uses `kiro-cli-chat` because it supports device-flow or SIGV4-based non-interactive use
    98→- The CLI transcript is only reliable at turn end because Kiro's SQLite conversation record is populated late
    99→- IDE prompt input may arrive through environment variables instead of stdin, so hook parsing must tolerate empty stdin
   100→- The native session ID from Kiro is not stable early in the turn, so Entire needs its own cached session ID file for hook coherence
   101→

1→# Kiro External Agent for Entire CLI
     2→
     3→Enables Entire CLI checkpoints, rewind, and transcript capture for [Kiro](https://kiro.dev) coding sessions. Once installed, Entire automatically tracks your Kiro sessions — creating checkpoints on commits and capturing transcripts for review.
     4→
     5→## Prerequisites
     6→
     7→- **Entire CLI** installed and on `PATH`
     8→- **Kiro** (IDE or `kiro-cli-chat` CLI) installed
     9→- **Go 1.26+** (to build from source)
    10→
    11→## Quick Start
    12→
    13→### 1. Build the binary
    14→
    15→```bash
    16→cd agents/entire-agent-kiro
    17→make build
    18→```
    19→
    20→This produces `./entire-agent-kiro` in the current directory.
    21→
    22→### 2. Install to PATH
    23→
    24→```bash
    25→cp ./entire-agent-kiro ~/.local/bin/
    26→```
    27→
    28→Or use Go install:
    29→
    30→```bash
    31→go install ./cmd/entire-agent-kiro
    32→```
    33→
    34→### 3. Verify the agent is discoverable
    35→
    36→```bash
    37→entire-agent-kiro info
    38→```
    39→
    40→This should print JSON describing the agent's capabilities.
    41→
    42→### 4. Enable the agent in your project
    43→
    44→```bash
    45→cd /path/to/your/project
    46→entire enable
    47→```
    48→
    49→### 5. Verify hooks are installed
    50→
    51→```bash
    52→entire-agent-kiro are-hooks-installed
    53→```
    54→
    55→Should return `{"installed": true}`.
    56→
    57→### 6. Start using Kiro
    58→
    59→Entire will now automatically capture checkpoints and transcripts during your Kiro sessions.
    60→
    61→## What Gets Installed
    62→
    63→When you run `entire enable --agent kiro`, the agent installs hooks in three locations:
    64→
    65→| Location | Purpose |
    66→|----------|---------|
    67→| `.kiro/agents/entire.json` | Agent configuration for Kiro CLI |
    68→| `.kiro/hooks/*.kiro.hook` | Lifecycle hooks (start, stop, commit) |
    69→| `.vscode/settings.json` | Trusted command entry for Kiro IDE |
    70→
    71→During a session, these hooks fire on lifecycle events (session start, stop, commit), allowing Entire to create checkpoints and capture what the AI agent did.
    72→
    73→## Capabilities
    74→
    75→| Capability | Supported | Description |
    76→|------------|-----------|-------------|
    77→| `hooks` | Yes | Installs and manages Kiro lifecycle hooks |
    78→| `transcript_analyzer` | Yes | Extracts modified files, prompts, and summaries from transcripts |
    79→| `transcript_preparer` | No | — |
    80→| `token_calculator` | No | — |
    81→| `text_generator` | No | — |
    82→| `hook_response_writer` | No | — |
    83→| `subagent_aware_extractor` | No | — |
    84→
    85→## Supported Subcommands
    86→
    87→All subcommands required by the [external agent protocol](https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md):
    88→
    89→**Core:** `info`, `detect`, `get-session-id`, `get-session-dir`, `resolve-session-file`, `read-session`, `write-session`, `format-resume-command`
    90→
    91→**Hooks:** `parse-hook`, `install-hooks`, `are-hooks-installed`, `uninstall-hooks`
    92→
    93→**Transcript:** `read-transcript`, `chunk-transcript`, `reassemble-transcript`, `get-transcript-position`, `extract-modified-files`, `extract-prompts`, `extract-summary`
    94→
    95→## Development
    96→
    97→```bash
    98→make build    # Build the binary
    99→make test     # Run unit tests
   100→make clean    # Remove built binary
   101→
   102→# Run directly without installing:
   103→go run ./cmd/entire-agent-kiro info
   104→```
   105→
   106→## E2E Tests
   107→
   108→E2E tests live in the shared `e2e/` directory at the repo root (not inside this agent's directory). The harness auto-discovers and builds all agents, then runs tests against each.
   109→
   110→### Subcommand tests (`e2e/kiro_test.go`)
   111→
   112→Exercise each protocol subcommand directly — no external dependencies needed:
   113→
   114→- **Identity**: `info`, `detect` (present/absent)
   115→- **Sessions**: `get-session-id`, `get-session-dir`, `resolve-session-file`, `write-session`/`read-session` round-trip
   116→- **Transcript**: `read-transcript`, `chunk-transcript`/`reassemble-transcript` round-trip
   117→- **Hooks**: `parse-hook` (spawn, prompt-submit, pre-tool-use, stop), `install-hooks`/`uninstall-hooks`/`are-hooks-installed`, idempotent install
   118→- **Transcript analysis**: `get-transcript-position`, `extract-modified-files`, `extract-prompts`, `extract-summary`
   119→- **Other**: `format-resume-command`, unknown subcommand handling
   120→
   121→### Lifecycle tests (`e2e/kiro_lifecycle_test.go`)
   122→
   123→Full integration tests requiring `entire` CLI and `kiro-cli-chat`:
   124→
   125→- **SinglePromptManualCommit** — agent creates file → commit → checkpoint with trailer
   126→- **MultiplePromptsManualCommit** — two prompts → single commit → checkpoint covers both
   127→- **DetectAndEnable** — `entire enable` succeeds when `.kiro/` exists
   128→- **HooksInstalledAfterEnable** — `are-hooks-installed` confirms hooks after enable
   129→- **RewindPreCommit** — create file A → checkpoint → create file B → rewind → B is gone
   130→- **RewindAfterCommit** — two commits → rewind to first → second file is gone
   131→- **SessionPersistence** — session file created in `.entire/tmp/` after prompt
   132→
   133→### Running
   134→
   135→```bash
   136→# From the repo root:
   137→make test-e2e                # All E2E tests (lifecycle tests skip if deps missing)
   138→make test-e2e-lifecycle      # Lifecycle tests only (fails if deps missing)
   139→
   140→# Run a specific test:
   141→cd e2e && go test -tags=e2e -v -count=1 -run TestKiro_Info ./...
   142→```
   143→
   144→## Troubleshooting
   145→
   146→**Agent not discovered by Entire**
   147→- Verify the binary is on your `PATH`: `which entire-agent-kiro`
   148→- Check detection: `entire-agent-kiro detect` (requires `ENTIRE_REPO_ROOT` to be set)
   149→
   150→**Hooks not firing**
   151→- Verify `.kiro/agents/entire.json` exists in your project
   152→- Check that `.kiro/hooks/` contains `*.kiro.hook` files
   153→- For Kiro IDE: verify `.vscode/settings.json` has the trusted command entry
   154→
   155→**IDE vs CLI differences**
   156→- Kiro IDE uses VS Code's trusted command mechanism — hooks fire via `.vscode/settings.json`
   157→- Kiro CLI (`kiro-cli-chat`) reads hooks directly from `.kiro/hooks/`
   158→- Both paths are configured by `install-hooks`
   159→
   160→## Protocol
   161→
   162→This agent implements the [Entire external agent protocol](https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md).

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me provide you with a thorough exploration report.

## Comprehensive E2E Tests & External Agents Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the external-agents project and can now provide you with detailed findings across all your requirements.

### 1. HOW E2E TESTS ARE RUN

**Test Execution**

The project uses Go's standard testing framework with build tags. Tests are run via:

```bash
# From project root Makefile:
make test-e2e                # Runs all E2E tests with -tags=e2e
make test-e2e-lifecycle      # Runs lifecycle tests only (with E2E_REQUIRE_LIFECYCLE=1)
make test-unit               # Unit tests for all agents
make test-all                # Everything
```

**Test Discovery & Building** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/e2e/setup_test.go`)

The `TestMain` function:
1. Discovers all agent directories matching pattern `agents/entire-agent-*` that have `cmd/<name>/main.go`
2. Builds each agent binary to a temporary directory
3. Prepends that directory to `PATH` so binaries are discoverable
4. Sets up artifact collection directory at `e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/`
5. Isolates git config to prevent user's `~/.gitconfig` from interfering
6. Performs preflight checks for dependencies like `tmux`

**Test Categories** (two tiers):

| Test Type | File | What it tests | Dependencies |
|-----------|------|---------------|--------------|
| **Protocol tests** | `e2e/kiro_test.go` | Individual agent subcommands (`info`, `detect`, `get-session-id`, `parse-hook`, etc.) via direct binary invocation | Agent binary only |
| **Lifecycle tests** | `e2e/kiro_lifecycle_test.go` | Full workflows: `entire enable` → prompt execution → git commit → checkpoint creation → rewind | `entire` CLI + agent CLI (e.g., `kiro-cli-chat`) |

**Environment Variables**

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `E2E_ENTIRE_BIN` | Path to entire binary (defaults to `entire` from PATH) |
| `E2E_REQUIRE_LIFECYCLE` | Set to `1` to fail (not skip) when lifecycle deps are missing |
| `E2E_AGENT` | Filter to a single agent (e.g., `kiro`) |
| `E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR` | Override artifact output directory |
| `E2E_KEEP_REPOS` | Preserve temp repos after tests |
| `E2E_CONCURRENT_TEST_LIMIT` | Override per-agent concurrency limit |

---

### 2. WHAT "EXTERNAL AGENT" MEANS IN THIS CODEBASE

**Definition** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/README.md`)

External agents are **standalone binaries** (named `entire-agent-<name>`) that teach the **Entire CLI** how to work with AI coding agents it doesn't natively support. They implement a well-defined protocol to integrate with Entire's checkpoint, transcript, and hook systems.

**How They Work**

1. **Auto-discovery**: When installed on `PATH`, Entire CLI automatically discovers and loads them
2. **Protocol-based**: They communicate with Entire via subcommands that accept/return JSON over stdin/stdout
3. **Three core capabilities**:
   - **Checkpoints**: Save repo state during AI sessions so you can rewind mistakes
   - **Transcripts**: Capture what the AI agent did and why
   - **Hooks**: Integrate with AI agent's lifecycle (start, stop, commit)

**Example: Kiro Agent** (`entire-agent-kiro`)

Located at `/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/agents/entire-agent-kiro/`

- **Binary name**: `entire-agent-kiro`
- **Underlying CLI**: `kiro-cli-chat` (the Kiro AI coding agent)
- **Hook locations**: 
  - `.kiro/agents/entire.json` (CLI hooks)
  - `.kiro/hooks/*.kiro.hook` (IDE hooks)
  - `.vscode/settings.json` (VS Code integration)
- **Session directory**: `.entire/tmp/` (normalized transcript cache)

**Protocol Spec**

The full external agent protocol is defined at: `https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`

Subcommands include:
- **Core**: `info`, `detect`, `get-session-id`, `get-session-dir`, `resolve-session-file`, `read-session`, `write-session`, `format-resume-command`
- **Hooks**: `parse-hook`, `install-hooks`, `uninstall-hooks`, `are-hooks-installed`
- **Transcript analysis**: `read-transcript`, `chunk-transcript`, `reassemble-transcript`, `get-transcript-position`, `extract-modified-files`, `extract-prompts`, `extract-summary`

---

### 3. CLAUDE CODE SETTINGS & PERMISSIONS

**Main Configuration** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/.claude/settings.json`)

Defines hooks for task lifecycle integration:
- `SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `Stop` hooks call `entire hooks claude-code` subcommands
- `PostToolUse`, `PreToolUse`, `TodoWrite`, `TaskCreate` hooks also fire

**Local Overrides** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/.claude/settings.local.json`)

```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Bash(find /Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/tests -type f ! -path */.git/*)"
    ]
  }
}
```

**Global Permissions** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/.claude/settings.json`)

```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "deny": [
      "Read(./.entire/metadata/**)"  # Prevents reading CI-generated metadata
    ]
  }
}
```

---

### 4. SKILL FILES & E2E INTEGRATION

**E2E Debug Skill** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/.claude/skills/e2e-debug/SKILL.md`)

Diagnostic skill for triaging E2E test failures using captured artifacts:

- **Input**: A test run directory (`e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/`) or specific test directory
- **Test categories**: Protocol tests vs. lifecycle tests
- **Artifact layout**:
  - `PASS`/`FAIL` status markers
  - `console.log`: Full operation transcript
  - `git-log.txt`, `git-tree.txt`: Git state
  - `entire-logs/entire.log`: Structured JSON logs
  - `checkpoint-metadata/`: Session and checkpoint metadata
  - `repo`: Symlink to preserved temp repo (if `E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1`)

- **Debugging workflow**: Triage → read console.log → read test source → diagnose issue

**Entire External Agent Skill** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md`)

Three-phase E2E-first TDD pipeline for building new external agents:

1. **Research** (`.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md`)
   - Analyze target agent's hook mechanism, transcript format, session management
   - Map native concepts to protocol subcommands
   - Produces `AGENT.md` with protocol mapping and E2E prerequisites

2. **Write-Tests** (`.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md`)
   - Scaffold binary with compilable stubs
   - Create self-contained E2E test harness
   - Tests expected to fail (they define the spec)

3. **Implement** (`.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md`)
   - Build real binary using strict E2E-first TDD
   - Run E2E tests at every step, implement minimum fix
   - Unit tests written last using real E2E data as fixtures

---

### 5. TEST HARNESS ARCHITECTURE

**Shared Test Infrastructure** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/e2e/`)

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `setup_test.go` | TestMain entry point — discovers agents, builds binaries, sets up PATH |
| `testenv.go` | TestEnv — isolated filesystem environment with agent binary runner |
| `harness.go` | AgentRunner — executes agent subcommands, captures output/exit codes |
| `fixtures.go` | Test input builders: HookInput, ParseHookInput, KiroTranscript |
| `entire/entire.go` | CLI wrappers: Enable, Disable, RewindList, Rewind |
| `agents/agent.go` | Agent interface + registry for all agents |
| `agents/kiro.go` | Kiro implementation: `kiro-cli-chat` runner with tmux support |
| `agents/tmux.go` | TmuxSession for interactive PTY sessions |
| `testutil/repo.go` | RepoState — single test's cloned repo with artifact capture |
| `testutil/metadata.go` | Checkpoint/session metadata types |
| `testutil/artifacts.go` | Artifact capture (git-log, pane, logs) |
| `testutil/assertions.go` | Test assertions (testify-based) |

**Test Flow**

1. **Setup**: `TestMain` builds all agents, sets up artifact dir
2. **Protocol Tests**: `AgentRunner` directly invokes agent subcommands with test inputs
3. **Lifecycle Tests**: `testutil.ForEachAgent()` runs test for each agent:
   - `SetupRepo()` creates temp git repo, runs `entire enable`, sets up artifact capture
   - Test runs agent prompts via `s.RunPrompt()`
   - `WaitForCheckpoint()` polls until checkpoint branch advances
   - Cleanup: `CaptureArtifacts()` writes all artifacts to disk

---

### 6. KIRO AGENT SPECIFICS

**Research Document** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/agents/entire-agent-kiro/AGENT.md`)

Comprehensive analysis of Kiro's hook mechanism and transcript format:

- **Hook config**: `.kiro/agents/entire.json` (CLI) and `.kiro/hooks/*.kiro.hook` (IDE)
- **Hook names**: `agentSpawn`, `userPromptSubmit`, `preToolUse`, `postToolUse`, `stop`
- **Session storage**: `.entire/tmp/<session-id>.json` normalized cache
- **Native lookups**: SQLite at `~/Library/Application Support/kiro-cli/data.sqlite3` (macOS)
- **Capabilities declared**: `hooks=true`, `transcript_analyzer=true`

**Test Implementation** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/e2e/agents/kiro.go`)

- **Registered**: `init()` registers Kiro when `E2E_AGENT` is unset or `"kiro"`

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me provide you with a thorough exploration report.

## Comprehensive E2E Tests & External Agents Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the external-agents project and can now provide you with detailed findings across all your requirements.

### 1. HOW E2E TESTS ARE RUN

**Test Execution**

The project uses Go's standard testing framework with build tags. Tests are run via:

```bash
# From project root Makefile:
make test-e2e                # Runs all E2E tests with -tags=e2e
make test-e2e-lifecycle      # Runs lifecycle tests only (with E2E_REQUIRE_LIFECYCLE=1)
make test-unit               # Unit tests for all agents
make test-all                # Everything
```

**Test Discovery & Building** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/e2e/setup_test.go`)

The `TestMain` function:
1. Discovers all agent directories matching pattern `agents/entire-agent-*` that have `cmd/<name>/main.go`
2. Builds each agent binary to a temporary directory
3. Prepends that directory to `PATH` so binaries are discoverable
4. Sets up artifact collection directory at `e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/`
5. Isolates git config to prevent user's `~/.gitconfig` from interfering
6. Performs preflight checks for dependencies like `tmux`

**Test Categories** (two tiers):

| Test Type | File | What it tests | Dependencies |
|-----------|------|---------------|--------------|
| **Protocol tests** | `e2e/kiro_test.go` | Individual agent subcommands (`info`, `detect`, `get-session-id`, `parse-hook`, etc.) via direct binary invocation | Agent binary only |
| **Lifecycle tests** | `e2e/kiro_lifecycle_test.go` | Full workflows: `entire enable` → prompt execution → git commit → checkpoint creation → rewind | `entire` CLI + agent CLI (e.g., `kiro-cli-chat`) |

**Environment Variables**

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `E2E_ENTIRE_BIN` | Path to entire binary (defaults to `entire` from PATH) |
| `E2E_REQUIRE_LIFECYCLE` | Set to `1` to fail (not skip) when lifecycle deps are missing |
| `E2E_AGENT` | Filter to a single agent (e.g., `kiro`) |
| `E2E_ARTIFACT_DIR` | Override artifact output directory |
| `E2E_KEEP_REPOS` | Preserve temp repos after tests |
| `E2E_CONCURRENT_TEST_LIMIT` | Override per-agent concurrency limit |

---

### 2. WHAT "EXTERNAL AGENT" MEANS IN THIS CODEBASE

**Definition** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/README.md`)

External agents are **standalone binaries** (named `entire-agent-<name>`) that teach the **Entire CLI** how to work with AI coding agents it doesn't natively support. They implement a well-defined protocol to integrate with Entire's checkpoint, transcript, and hook systems.

**How They Work**

1. **Auto-discovery**: When installed on `PATH`, Entire CLI automatically discovers and loads them
2. **Protocol-based**: They communicate with Entire via subcommands that accept/return JSON over stdin/stdout
3. **Three core capabilities**:
   - **Checkpoints**: Save repo state during AI sessions so you can rewind mistakes
   - **Transcripts**: Capture what the AI agent did and why
   - **Hooks**: Integrate with AI agent's lifecycle (start, stop, commit)

**Example: Kiro Agent** (`entire-agent-kiro`)

Located at `/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/agents/entire-agent-kiro/`

- **Binary name**: `entire-agent-kiro`
- **Underlying CLI**: `kiro-cli-chat` (the Kiro AI coding agent)
- **Hook locations**: 
  - `.kiro/agents/entire.json` (CLI hooks)
  - `.kiro/hooks/*.kiro.hook` (IDE hooks)
  - `.vscode/settings.json` (VS Code integration)
- **Session directory**: `.entire/tmp/` (normalized transcript cache)

**Protocol Spec**

The full external agent protocol is defined at: `https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md`

Subcommands include:
- **Core**: `info`, `detect`, `get-session-id`, `get-session-dir`, `resolve-session-file`, `read-session`, `write-session`, `format-resume-command`
- **Hooks**: `parse-hook`, `install-hooks`, `uninstall-hooks`, `are-hooks-installed`
- **Transcript analysis**: `read-transcript`, `chunk-transcript`, `reassemble-transcript`, `get-transcript-position`, `extract-modified-files`, `extract-prompts`, `extract-summary`

---

### 3. CLAUDE CODE SETTINGS & PERMISSIONS

**Main Configuration** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/.claude/settings.json`)

Defines hooks for task lifecycle integration:
- `SessionStart`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `Stop` hooks call `entire hooks claude-code` subcommands
- `PostToolUse`, `PreToolUse`, `TodoWrite`, `TaskCreate` hooks also fire

**Local Overrides** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/.claude/settings.local.json`)

```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Bash(find /Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/tests -type f ! -path */.git/*)"
    ]
  }
}
```

**Global Permissions** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/.claude/settings.json`)

```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "deny": [
      "Read(./.entire/metadata/**)"  # Prevents reading CI-generated metadata
    ]
  }
}
```

---

### 4. SKILL FILES & E2E INTEGRATION

**E2E Debug Skill** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/.claude/skills/e2e-debug/SKILL.md`)

Diagnostic skill for triaging E2E test failures using captured artifacts:

- **Input**: A test run directory (`e2e/artifacts/{timestamp}/`) or specific test directory
- **Test categories**: Protocol tests vs. lifecycle tests
- **Artifact layout**:
  - `PASS`/`FAIL` status markers
  - `console.log`: Full operation transcript
  - `git-log.txt`, `git-tree.txt`: Git state
  - `entire-logs/entire.log`: Structured JSON logs
  - `checkpoint-metadata/`: Session and checkpoint metadata
  - `repo`: Symlink to preserved temp repo (if `E2E_KEEP_REPOS=1`)

- **Debugging workflow**: Triage → read console.log → read test source → diagnose issue

**Entire External Agent Skill** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/SKILL.md`)

Three-phase E2E-first TDD pipeline for building new external agents:

1. **Research** (`.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/research.md`)
   - Analyze target agent's hook mechanism, transcript format, session management
   - Map native concepts to protocol subcommands
   - Produces `AGENT.md` with protocol mapping and E2E prerequisites

2. **Write-Tests** (`.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/write-tests.md`)
   - Scaffold binary with compilable stubs
   - Create self-contained E2E test harness
   - Tests expected to fail (they define the spec)

3. **Implement** (`.claude/skills/entire-external-agent/implement.md`)
   - Build real binary using strict E2E-first TDD
   - Run E2E tests at every step, implement minimum fix
   - Unit tests written last using real E2E data as fixtures

---

### 5. TEST HARNESS ARCHITECTURE

**Shared Test Infrastructure** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/e2e/`)

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `setup_test.go` | TestMain entry point — discovers agents, builds binaries, sets up PATH |
| `testenv.go` | TestEnv — isolated filesystem environment with agent binary runner |
| `harness.go` | AgentRunner — executes agent subcommands, captures output/exit codes |
| `fixtures.go` | Test input builders: HookInput, ParseHookInput, KiroTranscript |
| `entire/entire.go` | CLI wrappers: Enable, Disable, RewindList, Rewind |
| `agents/agent.go` | Agent interface + registry for all agents |
| `agents/kiro.go` | Kiro implementation: `kiro-cli-chat` runner with tmux support |
| `agents/tmux.go` | TmuxSession for interactive PTY sessions |
| `testutil/repo.go` | RepoState — single test's cloned repo with artifact capture |
| `testutil/metadata.go` | Checkpoint/session metadata types |
| `testutil/artifacts.go` | Artifact capture (git-log, pane, logs) |
| `testutil/assertions.go` | Test assertions (testify-based) |

**Test Flow**

1. **Setup**: `TestMain` builds all agents, sets up artifact dir
2. **Protocol Tests**: `AgentRunner` directly invokes agent subcommands with test inputs
3. **Lifecycle Tests**: `testutil.ForEachAgent()` runs test for each agent:
   - `SetupRepo()` creates temp git repo, runs `entire enable`, sets up artifact capture
   - Test runs agent prompts via `s.RunPrompt()`
   - `WaitForCheckpoint()` polls until checkpoint branch advances
   - Cleanup: `CaptureArtifacts()` writes all artifacts to disk

### 6. KIRO AGENT SPECIFICS

**Research Document** (`/Users/alisha/Projects/external-agents/agents/entire-agent-kiro/AGENT.md`)

Comprehensive analysis of Kiro's hook mechanism and transcript format:

1→---
     2→name: implement
     3→description: >
     4→  Phase 3: Build the external agent binary using strict E2E-first TDD.
     5→  Use /entire-external-agent implement or /entire-external-agent:implement
     6→  when you only need the implementation phase.
     7→---
     8→
     9→# Implement Procedure
    10→
    11→Build the external agent binary using strict E2E-first TDD. E2E tests drive development at every step — run each tier, watch it fail, implement the minimum fix, repeat. Unit tests are written only after all E2E tiers pass, using real data from E2E runs as golden fixtures.
    12→
    13→> **Warning:** This phase involves iterative E2E test cycles with real agent invocations. Expect this to take 2-4 hours depending on agent complexity and API response times.
    14→
    15→## Prerequisites
    16→
    17→Ensure the following are available:
    18→- `AGENT_NAME`, `AGENT_SLUG`, `LANGUAGE`, `PROJECT_DIR` — from orchestrator or user
    19→- `<PROJECT_DIR>/AGENT.md` — research one-pager with E2E test prerequisites
    20→- Scaffolded project that compiles and responds to `info`
    21→- E2E test harness at `<PROJECT_DIR>/e2e/` that compiles
    22→
    23→## Core Principle: E2E-First TDD
    24→
    25→1. **E2E tests are the spec.** The `e2e/` test harness defines what "working" means. You implement until tests pass.
    26→2. **Watch it fail first.** Every E2E tier starts by running the test and observing the failure. If you haven't seen the failure, you don't understand what needs fixing.
    27→3. **Minimum viable fix.** At each failure, implement only the code needed to make that specific assertion pass. Don't anticipate future tiers.
    28→4. **No unit tests during Steps 3-9.** Unit tests are written in Step 11 after all E2E tiers pass, using real data from E2E runs as golden fixtures.
    29→5. **Format and lint, don't unit test.** Between E2E tiers, run format/lint to keep code clean. No unit tests between tiers.
    30→6. **If you didn't watch it fail, you don't know if it tests the right thing.**
    31→
    32→**Do NOT write unit tests during Steps 3-9.** All unit test writing is consolidated in Step 11.
    33→
    34→## Procedure
    35→
    36→### Step 1: Read Protocol Spec + AGENT.md
    37→
    38→Read these files before writing any code:
    39→
    40→1. Read `https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/docs/architecture/external-agent-protocol.md` — full protocol spec
    41→2. Read `https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/cmd/entire/cli/agent/external/types.go` — JSON response types
    42→3. Read `https://github.com/entireio/cli/blob/main/cmd/entire/cli/agent/external/external.go` — how the CLI calls each subcommand
    43→4. Read `<PROJECT_DIR>/AGENT.md` — agent-specific hook mechanism, transcript format, config structure, E2E prerequisites
    44→
    45→### Step 2: Verify Baseline
    46→
    47→Build the binary and run the first E2E test to confirm it fails for the right reason (agent behavior, not harness bug).
    48→
    49→```bash
    50→make build && make install
    51→make test:e2e:run TEST=TestHookInstallAndDetect
    52→```
    53→
    54→**Expected:** Test fails because the agent binary returns stub data. If the test fails for a different reason (harness compilation error, missing binary, broken assertion), fix the harness first.
    55→
    56→### Step 3: E2E Tier 1 — `TestHookInstallAndDetect`
    57→
    58→**What it exercises:**
    59→- `detect` — agent binary detection
    60→- `install-hooks` — hook installation via `entire enable`
    61→- `are-hooks-installed` — hook presence detection
    62→- Basic binary invocation and JSON response format
    63→
    64→**Cycle:**
    65→
    66→1. Run: `make test:e2e:run TEST=TestHookInstallAndDetect`
    67→2. **Watch it FAIL** — read the failure output carefully
    68→3. Read the failure — what subcommand/behavior is missing?
    69→4. Implement the MINIMUM code to fix the failure
    70→5. Re-run until PASS
    71→6. `make build`
    72→7. Commit
    73→
    74→### Step 4: E2E Tier 2 — `TestSingleSessionManualCommit`
    75→
    76→The foundational test. Exercises the full agent lifecycle: start session → agent prompt → agent produces files → user commits → checkpoint created.
    77→
    78→**What it exercises:**
    79→- `parse-hook` for all event types (session start, turn start, turn end, session end)
    80→- `get-session-id` — session ID extraction from hook input
    81→- `get-session-dir` / `resolve-session-file` — finding session/transcript files
    82→- `read-session` / `write-session` — session data management
    83→- `read-transcript` / `chunk-transcript` / `reassemble-transcript` — transcript handling
    84→
    85→**Cycle:**
    86→
    87→1. Run: `make test:e2e:run TEST=TestSingleSessionManualCommit`
    88→2. **Watch it FAIL** — read the failure output carefully
    89→3. Read the failure — which subcommand returns wrong data or errors?
    90→4. Implement the MINIMUM code to fix the failure
    91→5. Re-run until PASS
    92→6. `make build`
    93→7. Commit
    94→
    95→### Step 5: E2E Tier 3 — `TestCheckpointDeepValidation`
    96→
    97→Validates transcript quality: JSONL validity, content hash correctness, prompt extraction accuracy.
    98→
    99→**What it exercises:**
   100→- `get-transcript-position` — transcript file size/position
   101→- `extract-modified-files` — parsing transcript for file operations
   102→- `extract-prompts` — parsing transcript for user messages
   103→- `extract-summary` — parsing transcript for AI summaries
   104→
   105→**Cycle:**
   106→
   107→1. Run: `make test:e2e:run TEST=TestCheckpointDeepValidation`
   108→2. **Watch it FAIL** — this test often exposes subtle transcript formatting bugs
   109→3. Implement the MINIMUM fix
   110→4. Re-run until PASS
   111→5. `make build`
   112→6. Commit
   113→
   114→### Step 6: E2E Tier 4 — `TestMultipleTurnsManualCommit`
   115→
   116→Multi-turn session management. Two sequential prompts, one commit.
   117→
   118→**What it exercises:**
   119→- Session persistence across multiple prompts
   120→- Transcript accumulation across turns
   121→- Checkpoint capturing both turns
   122→
   123→**Cycle:**
   124→
   125→1. Run: `make test:e2e:run TEST=TestMultipleTurnsManualCommit`
   126→2. **Watch it FAIL**
   127→3. Implement the MINIMUM fix
   128→4. Re-run until PASS

   130→6. Commit

   132→### Step 7: E2E Tier 5 — `TestSessionMetadata`
